title: Chaetogastra cardinalis
intro: Chaetogastra cardinalis, also known as Cardinal's Feather, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its distinctive red or purple flowers and feathery foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a promising remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Chaetogastra cardinalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chaetogastra cardinalis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Chaetogastra cardinalis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the tribe Mutisieae within the family.
